title: rubus × entomodontoides
intro: Rubus × entomodontoides, also known as the hybrid blackberry or thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Rubus argutus and Rubus laciniatus, two species of blackberry plants. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus × entomodontoides include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications to take advantage of its flavor and nutritional properties.
